name: smart-money-concepts
description: Discover our Python package designed for algorithmic trading. It brings ICT's smart money concepts to Python, offering a range of indicators for your algorithmic trading strategies.
readme_summary: This Python package implements Inner Circle Trader (ICT) smart money concepts for algorithmic trading. It provides a suite of indicators like Order Blocks, Liquidity, Fair Value Gaps, and market structure breaks to help traders analyze market sentiment and identify potential trading opportunities.
key_features: Break of Structure (BOS) and Change of Character (CHoCH) indicators
primary_use_case: To provide traders with Python-based tools to implement and backtest algorithmic trading strategies using Smart Money Concepts (SMC) and ICT methodologies.
cover_image_prompt: A seasoned financial cartographer meticulously drawing a treasure map on an ancient parchment. Instead of islands and seas, the map depicts complex financial market charts with glowing lines representing 'liquidity' and 'order blocks'. The cartographer uses a magnifying glass to pinpoint 'fair value gaps' and a compass to mark 'swing highs and lows'. Subtle digital particles emanate from the map, hinting at algorithmic analysis. The repository name 'smart-money-concepts' is subtly embossed on the cartographer's tools. The scene is set in a dimly lit, sophisticated study, emphasizing discovery and precision. The style is a detailed digital painting with a focus on clear iconography and a rich, deep color palette.

name: pytermgui
description: Python TUI framework with mouse support, modular widget system, customizable and rapid terminal markup language and more!
readme_summary: PyTermGUI is a Python framework for building rich and interactive terminal user interfaces (TUIs). It offers a modular widget system, mouse support, and a customizable markup language for styling text, enabling the creation of desktop-like applications within the terminal.
key_features: Customizable and rapid terminal markup language (TIM)
SVG and HTML screenshots of terminal output
primary_use_case: Developing complex, visually appealing, and interactive terminal user interfaces with ease.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical alchemist in a dimly lit, cozy workshop, surrounded by bubbling beakers and intricate machinery. The alchemist is carefully pouring a stream of raw, unrefined data (represented as glowing digital particles) from a large flask into a complex, ornate terminal console. The console, in turn, is outputting a beautifully rendered, vibrant 3D isometric cityscape that seems to float in the air above it. Subtle lines of Python code are etched into the workshop's wooden beams, and the PyTermGUI logo is subtly integrated into a decorative element on the console. The overall mood is one of creative transformation and magical engineering, rendered in a detailed digital painting style with a focus on glowing elements and clear visual storytelling.

name: PyPDFForm
description: :fire: The Python library for PDF forms.
readme_summary: PyPDFForm is a Python library designed for automating PDF form interactions. It allows users to programmatically inspect, fill, and create form fields within PDF documents, simplifying form processing tasks.
key_features: Fill PDF forms using Python dictionaries
Create new form fields on PDFs
primary_use_case: Automating the process of filling and managing data within PDF forms using Python.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, anthropomorphic digital assistant, resembling a stylized PDF document with helpful icons, is efficiently sorting and filling out numerous paper forms laid out on a large, clean desk. The assistant uses a glowing pen to instantly fill in fields, with data streams flowing from a nearby laptop displaying simple Python code. Input forms are on the left, and perfectly filled output forms are neatly stacked on the right. Subtle glowing lines connect the laptop to the forms, indicating data transfer. The overall mood is one of organized efficiency and digital automation. The style should be a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration with a bright, inviting color palette.

name: cursor-clip
description: Cursor Clip - GTK4 Clipboard Manager with dynamic positioning. Features a Windows 11–style clipboard history, adapted to native GNOME design.
readme_summary: Cursor Clip is a modern Wayland clipboard manager built with Rust and GTK4, offering a Windows 11-style clipboard history that dynamically positions itself at the mouse cursor. It integrates seamlessly with the GNOME design language and Wayland protocols for reliable clipboard handling.
key_features: Advanced Wayland integration (Layer Shell Protocol, Cursor Tracking)
Automatic clipboard monitoring and content classification (text, URLs, code, files, images)
Quick selection, pin, and delete history items
primary_use_case: To provide users with a visually intuitive and dynamically positioned clipboard history manager on Wayland compositors, enhancing productivity and clipboard management.
cover_image_prompt: A magical quill pen hovering dynamically at the tip of a user's finger, instantly capturing snippets of information (text, images, file icons) that float around it. These captured snippets then arrange themselves into a neat, organized scroll that unfurls just below the quill, displaying a history of recent captures with clear icons and timestamps. The background is a clean, modern desktop interface with subtle digital particles flowing from the quill to the scroll. The overall mood is one of effortless organization and enhanced productivity. The repository name 'Cursor Clip' is subtly etched onto the quill's handle. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a clean, vibrant color palette.
